notes: All 40 campaign batches completed. Two initial AT synchronization timeouts on measured COM43, at TX -6 dBm / 2 Mbps / 8 B and 16 B, recovered automatically on attempt two; all accepted results are complete.
All 180 packet events were detected by PPK2. Sixty-four packets were not delivered over RF and are retained as rx_missing observations rather than measurement failures.

Two continuous RX rows legitimately report no_rx_data: the peer offered frames for 60 seconds, transmitter serial errors were zero, PPK2 captured the full measured window, and RF delivery was 0/3366 at 250 kbps for both -6 and 0 dBm.
A 36-scenario serial-only diagnostic reproduced the power/rate-dependent loss in both directions and on channels 80 and 40 with zero serial errors, excluding PPK2 and the web campaign as the cause.
The expected nRF24 RF_SETUP values were read back on both radios. Disabling the internal LNA did not improve the link, so simple internal LNA saturation is not supported by the diagnostic.

The highest campaign packet-energy coefficient of variation, 35.053985%, occurred at TX 16 B / -18 dBm / 2 Mbps. A separate 30-capture recovery repeated the variability with stable peaks but variable pulse duration, confirming a repeatable PA/front-end timing or supply behavior rather than a PPK2 trigger failure.

The leading bench-level hypotheses are external PA/LNA front-end behavior, local supply/decoupling integrity, and close-range RF coupling. The dataset is accepted as the authoritative baseline for this pair and layout, not as a clean-link reference.

Post-validation cleanup removes 42 non-authoritative quick/recovery raw traces after gzip CRC verification and compact-result archival. All 192 authoritative campaign traces remain present and unchanged.
